reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m fully confident in my own Q1 answer (35 ÷ 5), and across all recent games B and C have never contributed a wrong capsule, while D’s Q4 has been correct repeatedly and often verified. With only one simple arithmetic capsule per question and no conflicts, the chance that this full password is wrong is very small—much smaller than the 1/4 random-death risk if nobody volunteers. Verifying now would cost a coin and only marginally increase confidence, so the best move is to volunteer using Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: My puzzle (30 - 12) is simple and I’m sure my own capsule is correct, but that only matters if I’m the one entering the password. In early games, Round 1 often had no volunteer and a random player died, which made volunteering the safer move. Recently, though, A has started reliably volunteering in Round 1 and we’ve had no random deaths; all passwords built from the single-endorsed capsules have been correct.

If I volunteer, I risk dying if any of the four capsules is wrong; we’ve seen at least one wrong initial answer across many puzzles, so that risk isn’t negligible. If I pass, my only real danger is the case where, unlike the last several games, nobody volunteers and a random player is killed (25% chance it’s me). Given A’s strong recent tendency to step up, that no-volunteer scenario now seems unlikely, so passing gives me a lower expected death risk than volunteering. Verifying would cost a coin and mainly helps the volunteer’s safety, not mine, so I’ll wait and rely on others—especially A—to volunteer.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own capsule for Q3 is 33+27=60, which I’m fully confident in. All the questions in these games have been very simple arithmetic, and aside from one early slip by D on Q4 (which has since been repeatedly correct and verified), everyone’s answers have almost always been right. 

If I don’t volunteer, I risk the “everyone hesitates” scenario where no one volunteers and a player is killed at random with a 1-in-4 chance it’s me—this has already happened in a previous game. By volunteering, I trade that large random-death risk for the much smaller chance that one of the other three straightforward answers is wrong. With no conflicting capsules and a strong track record of correct arithmetic, it’s safer overall for me to volunteer using the single available capsule for each question.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
